name: Buitrago de Lozolla, Puebla de la Sierra, Horcajo de la Sierra – circular route
name_original: Buitrago de Lozolla, Puebla de la Sierra, Horcajo de la Sierra – ruta circular
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes around Pantano de Puentes Viejas are set within the Sierra Norte of Madrid, offering diverse terrain for road cyclists. The region is characterized by the tranquil waters of the Pantano de Puentes Viejas reservoir, fed by the Lozoya River, and features "serrano" landscapes and "dehesas." Cyclists encounter varied natural settings, including the Bosque de las Gariñas and the Pinar de Buitrago, providing a mix of forested and open areas. The area's well-maintained paved surfaces cater to road cycling, with routes ranging from moderate to difficult. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Pantano de Puentes Viejas The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Buitrago de Lozolla, Puebla de la Sierra, Horcajo de la Sierra – circular route, a 59.6 miles (95.9 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores a wide area, passing through several towns and varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de la Puebla Loop, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, offering challenging climbs and panoramic views from the Puerto de la Puebla. Local road cyclists also love the El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – El Villar Reservoir Dam loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, a 37.4 miles (60.3 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes and a gorge, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Pantano de Puentes Viejas is defined by reservoir views, river valleys, and forested sections like the Bosque de las Gariñas and Pinar de Buitrago. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating various ability levels. The routes in Pantano de Puentes Viejas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 4,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pantano de Puentes Viejas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: There are over 35 dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes around Pantano de Puentes Viejas, offering a wide range of options for cyclists looking for quiet roads.

Q: What is the general terrain and elevation like for no-traffic road cycling around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: The terrain around Pantano de Puentes Viejas is varied, featuring picturesque landscapes with both rolling hills and more challenging climbs. You can expect significant elevation changes on many routes, with some reaching over 1,800 meters of ascent, offering a rewarding experience for those seeking a workout.

Q: Are there challenging no-traffic road cycling routes available for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenging no-traffic routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977462401/buitrago-de-lozolla-puebla-de-la-sierra-horcajo-de-la-sierra-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buitrago de Lozolla, Puebla de la Sierra, Horcajo de la Sierra – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult option spanning over 95 km with more than 1,800 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977458933/puerto-de-la-puebla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Puebla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 66 km with over 1,200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there easier no-traffic road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a more relaxed ride?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buitrago de Lozoya – La Serna del Monte loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 38 km route with around 500 meters of elevation, offering a less strenuous experience while still enjoying the scenery.

Q: What natural features will I encounter while cycling around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: You'll cycle through diverse natural settings, including the tranquil waters of the Pantano de Puentes Viejas reservoir and along the meandering &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2559384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lozoya River&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often pass through leafy forests like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de las Gariñas&lt;/a&gt;, abundant with pine, ash, and Montpellier maple trees, as well as 'serrano' landscapes and 'dehesas' (wooded pastures).

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentes Viejas Dam&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the historic medieval town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4646043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, known for its completely preserved town wall and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4646044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Mendoza Family&lt;/a&gt;. These provide excellent points of interest and photo opportunities along your ride.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes around Pantano de Puentes Viejas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24388434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Puebla – Buitrago de Lozoya loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural beauty, the quiet, traffic-free roads perfect for focused riding, and the rewarding climbs that offer panoramic views of the reservoirs and surrounding mountains.

Q: Is there parking available near the starting points of these no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, towns like Buitrago del Lozoya, which serve as common starting points for many routes, typically offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check local parking options in advance, especially during peak seasons, to ensure a smooth start to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for traffic-free road cycling around Pantano de Puentes Viejas?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially midday, while winter can bring colder temperatures, though clear days are still excellent for riding.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the medieval town of Buitrago del Lozoya, the region also features historical remnants such as the partially ruined 17th-century Casa del Bosque and even Civil War bunkers, adding a layer of historical interest to your rides.
tips: This dam offers spectacular views. It is worth going there. Ideal for hiking, motorbike or road bike excursions. Next to the dam there is a small parking lot for about 10 cars.

On the Puebla de la Sierra highway you come across this viewpoint over the Puebla river gorge. The landscape is overwhelming. The vertical walls of bare rock stand before the green ridges of the mountains and the sky. It is an ideal place to immortalize with a photograph.
La Puebla, a port with two slopes, has a rough start but before the second kilometer it softens.
The surroundings of this Madrid town are an extraordinary place to go on a bike route, especially during autumn, due to the abundance of deciduous trees.

The Castle and Walls of Buitrago del Lozoya, one of the most important medieval sites in the Community of Madrid:
The town has pre-Roman roots and was mentioned by Livy as a Celtiberian fortress (Litabrum).
Muslim Fortification: Between the 8th and 11th centuries, the Muslims built the wall to protect the territory.
Reconquest: Alfonso VI conquered it in 1085, and it passed into Christian hands.
Current Castle: Built in the 15th century by the Mendoza family, during the reign of John II of Castile.
Historical Uses: Noble residence, defensive stronghold, and setting for events such as the imprisonment of Juana la Beltraneja.

waypoints.name: Buitrago del Lozoya Castle and Walls
